title: File Servers, Object Storage, CDNs, and Architecture Selection · Execution Trace: Control Data Plane
summary: A product sends every byte through one web process, stores uploads on an ephemeral disk, and adds a CDN without deciding authority, mutation, privacy, or failure boundaries. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the control data plane increment in architecture and responsibility decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Separate control-plane metadata and authorization from high-volume data transfer while preserving one auditable decision.
Compare local filesystems, shared filesystems, object storage, databases, origin services, and CDNs by interface, consistency, scale, trust, and operations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Control Data Plane · Theory Control Data Plane
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 5214 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a file, object, gateway, direct, or CDN path from trust and volume evidence; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Control Data Plane contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Control Data Plane · Theory Control Data Plane.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate control-plane metadata and authorization from high-volume data transfer while preserving one auditable decision. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Control Data Plane · Theory Control Data Plane, keep Control Data Plane connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

options.text: All bytes must traverse the application server for the application to retain control. That claim fails the predict control data plane evidence boundary for Control Data Plane.

For predict control data plane, run one convenient Control Data Plane happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output. This does not satisfy Control Data Plane for architecture and responsibility decision record.
Separate control-plane metadata and authorization from high-volume data transfer while preserving one auditable decision. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Bound Control Data Plane · Theory Control Data Plane
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 5023 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a file, object, gateway, direct, or CDN path from trust and volume evidence; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate control-plane metadata and authorization from high-volume data transfer while preserving one auditable decision.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Control Data Plane application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.

content.text: Separate control-plane metadata and authorization from high-volume data transfer while preserving one auditable decision. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name stakeholder and file identity, object key and version, representation, tenant and authorization, byte, time, attempt, concurrency, cache, and cost budgets, custody and state, checksum, cancellation, cleanup, rollback, and recovery; run a pure deterministic Go model; then change one file or failure and state the Go compiler, race, leak, controlled AWS SDK, S3, CloudFront, IAM, KMS, WAF, load, cost, restore, or production transfer gate. Apply that move specifically to Control Data Plane while producing architecture and responsibility decision record.

steps.instruction:
